title: European company sells planes to Myanmar junta despite sanctions
subhed: An investigation by Le Monde and the Centre for Information Resilience has uncovered the deliveries of four aircraft from a Czech company to the military dictatorship, despite an EU ban on the export of weapons and goods that could be used for military purposes.

China's execution of a Frenchman convicted of drug trafficking casts a chill on relations with Paris
subhed: Chan Thao Phoumy was sentenced in 2010 after authorities dismantled a methamphetamine trafficking network. France has condemned the fact that his defense was not allowed to attend his final court hearing.

title: How Morocco is renewing its influence networks in France
subhed: Some of Morocco's traditional networks of influence in France have been put on hold in recent years. How they might be restructured remains uncertain, while new voices have struggled to replace high-profile figures.

title: Pope Leo urges 'those who have the power to unleash wars' to 'choose peace'
subhed: In his first Easter blessing as pontiff, the pope also spoke of 'a world ravaged by wars and marked by a hatred and indifference that make us feel powerless in the face of evil.'

title: Looking for Cécile, a 'bad girl' in France's sinister former 'preservation schools'
subhed: Until the mid-20th century, correctional facilities run by the French prison administration held minors accused of theft or 'debauchery' in appalling conditions. Upon his retirement, Michel Bey discovered that his mother, Cécile, who died when he was 5, was one of them.

Fighter jet shot down in Iran, purges within the top ranks: The US military is in turmoil
subhed: Two days after Donald Trump declared that Tehran was nearly defeated, the US was still trying to locate one of the crew members of an F-15E who had to eject over Iran. Meanwhile, Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth continued to carry out a series of dismissals, unrelated to the conflict.

title: Pre-election Hungary's mood is one of twilight for Orban's rule
subhed: After 16 years of unchallenged rule, Hungary's ultra-conservative prime minister is, for the first time, seeing his supremacy challenged. With days until the April 12 legislative elections, pro-European challenger Peter Magyar is leading in the polls. In the ruling party's eastern strongholds, huge Chinese battery factories have sparked public backlash.

title: New Saint-Denis mayor seeks to turn racist insults against him into 'a foundation for building things'
subhed: Bally Bagayoko, the new hard-left mayor of the northern Paris suburb, filed a complaint for 'public insults' of a racist nature on Thursday, after having been targeted by racist insults on the CNews channel since being elected. He has called for a large anti-racist rally to be held in his city on Saturday.
